title: EFT: Self-Soothing
description: Three practical clinician-facing slides extracted from the EFT interventions practice deck.

sources.label: Vrana and Greenberg, Overview of Emotion-Focused Therapy

slides.title: Marker: pain without an inner responder
slides.body: Use when vulnerability is present but no caring voice is available.
bullets: Client is in primary vulnerability, not performative collapse.
Need is specific: comfort, protection, validation, holding, permission.
Therapist offers the task as an experiment, not reassurance.

slides.title: Run the task from felt need, not technique
slides.body: Experienced clients often know self-compassion language; the intervention works when the need becomes bodily and addressed.
steps: Deepen the vulnerable self: what hurts, where, and what is missing.
Differentiate the needed response from advice, analysis, or cheerleading.
Invite a soothing self, caring other, or protective presence into the other chair.
Have the responder speak directly: slow, concrete, second person.
Return to the vulnerable side and check whether the body receives it.

slides.title: Clinician tracking
rows: Ask for the words the hurt part can actually take in.
Narrow to one need and one sentence.
Borrow the therapist voice, then hand it back.
